question_text: State the SI unit used to measure density.
correct_answer: kilograms per cubic metre (kg/m³) or kg m⁻³
explanation: Density measures how much mass is packed into a certain volume. The SI unit for density is kilograms per cubic metre (kg/m³). This means it tells you how many kilograms of material fit into one cubic metre of space. In some KS3 contexts, you might also see grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³) used, but kg/m³ is the official SI unit.
